data.body:
User feedback from /taxonomize review (2026-04-16): States are authored in loom workflow bundles under loom/*/. KnotState at src/domain/state.rs:6 is currently hand-maintained and must stay in lockstep with every loom workflow. That is a drift risk — any state we reference in code should be generated from loom, not hand-written.

Proposal:
- Treat loom/*/dist/bundle.json as the source of truth for state vocabulary.
- Add a build step (probably invoked via make loom-bundle or a new make target) that emits the canonical KnotState enum (and possibly state-owner/queue/action classification) from the union of all bundles.
- Remove hand-edited variants from src/domain/state.rs; make it a generated file with a clear generation header.
- Ensure any renames or additions in loom/*/workflow.loom propagate automatically.

Acceptance:
- KnotState is generated, not hand-edited.
- make sanity verifies that src/domain/state.rs is in sync with loom bundles (fail-on-drift).
- Any new state added in loom appears in KnotState after a single build step.

Risk: multiple workflows use overlapping state names (e.g. ready_for_planning appears in 4 bundles). The generator must deduplicate by name.
data.title: Generate KnotState (and all state vocabulary) from loom bundles
